title: QUALCOMM (QCOM) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.0% aligns with mature semiconductor industry growth, slightly pacing global GDP. As computing moves from the cloud to the edge (phones, cars, appliances), Qualcomm's IP will remain foundational, allowing them to grow steadily in perpetuity without massive CapEx overhauls.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: The End of Handset Dominance is the Beginning of Scale
thesisParagraphs: Qualcomm's historical narrative has been tied heavily to the global smartphone replacement cycle and its high-margin QTL (Qualcomm Technology Licensing) business. As the smartphone market has reached saturation, many analysts expect Qualcomm's growth to stagnate. I see a company at the perfect inflection point of a massive TAM expansion.

The successful launch of the Snapdragon X Elite for Windows PCs and aggressive design wins in the automotive sector (ADAS and digital chassis) represent high-margin, sticky revenue streams. My valuation heavily weights the success of this diversification strategy. This isn't just about selling modems anymore; it's about powering the compute layer at the edge for the AI era.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project a 10% FCF growth rate for Qualcomm?
faq.answer: Gemini projects that while the legacy smartphone market is maturing, Qualcomm's aggressive and successful expansion into automotive chips and the emerging AI PC market (Snapdragon X Elite) will drive a sustained 10% free cash flow growth over the next five years.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for Qualcomm's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.8% discount rate was used. This incorporates a 4.18% risk-free rate and a 1.267 Beta. This rate reflects the inherent cyclicality of the semiconductor sector while acknowledging the stability provided by Qualcomm's high-margin technology licensing business (QTL).

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.
